what_it_does: Stores a structured array of design-pattern findings extracted from multiple pitch-deck and landing-page artifacts; each entry records a named pattern, its category, a recipe, observed evidence, a prescriptive generator rule, source artifact, and compression depth.
observations: Each finding pairs an observed design behavior with an imperative generator rule.
Categories include sequence, attention-mechanism, attention-direction, emergent-attention-currency, and emergent-attention-directing.
Evidence is usually given as slide or page references (e.g., p-02, lp2 strip_02) rather than quantitative data.
The file spans many source artifacts and compression depths: 1-page, 15-page, 18-page, 30-page, 49-page, and multi-section landing pages.
Some findings are flagged emergent: true while others are emergent: false.
Generator rules mix visual design, copywriting, layout, and narrative sequencing instructions.
The file is truncated near the end; the last case-study generator rule is cut off.
standouts: Gold/amber is treated as the sole attention and action signal across all artifacts.
Slide titles are authored as connected clauses forming a continuous spoken-pitch sentence.
The same content is intentionally compressed across 1-page, 15-page, and 30-page/49-page depths while preserving grammar.
The appendix is reconceived as an embedded, self-sufficient second deck rather than supplementary material.
conventions: Consistent schema keys: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent, generator_rule, _src, _depth.
Generator rules use imperative voice and absolute adverbs (always, never, must).
Color semantics are encoded with hex values and explicit role assignments.
Source and depth metadata are attached to every finding.
Evidence is typically a short reference list, sometimes marked as inferred.
concerns: The file is truncated, so the final generator rule and any following findings are incomplete.
Some evidence claims are inferred rather than directly observed.
Generator rules occasionally blend design, copy, and content-strategy responsibilities without separating concerns.
notes: A design-language ledger that turns observed pitch-deck patterns into generative rules organized by attention, sequence, and compression.
questions: What determines whether a finding is marked emergent: true versus false?
How are conflicts between generator rules from different source artifacts resolved?
Is this file intended for human designers or for an automated generator?
What is the full text of the truncated final entry?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to be a pattern-extraction output meant to feed a generative design system; it does that, though it also reads like a design critique and rulebook, blurring documentation and instruction.
apparent_themes: Attention economics: a single gold accent routes all visual priority.
Narrative sequencing: slide order and layout mode carry the argument.
Compression invariance: design grammar survives across page-depth variants.
Audience-adaptive grammar: layout changes based on professional versus lifestyle audience intent.
Repetition as rhetoric: template reuse and mirrored bookends communicate scalability and closure.
intention_signals: The file is trying to extract a reusable, generative design grammar from a family of pitch decks and landing pages so that future artifacts can be produced at any compression depth while preserving the brand's attention and narrative logic.
novelty: It treats pitch-deck structure as a formal language with clause-level title grammar, arrow-direction semantics, and compression-invariant atoms, rather than as a collection of one-off slides.
contribution: A cross-artifact catalog of design rules that links visual choices such as color, arrows, scale, and layout to rhetorical functions, giving a fused system a vocabulary for generating consistent pitch and landing-page sequences.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON ledger of design-pattern findings extracted from pitch decks and landing pages, each pairing observed behavior with a generator rule for attention, sequence, and compression.
intention_for_embedding: To capture a reusable generative grammar for producing consistent pitch-deck and landing-page artifacts across multiple compression depths and audiences.
